title: Elegant Crisp Brazilian Pepper Salad
description: A garlicky, crisp salad that showcases roasted bell peppers and a garlic-infused oil, perfect for a Brazilian-inspired, diabetic-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper, roasted and diced

instructions: Slice the bell peppers into strips, toss them with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of black pepper, then spread them on a baking sheet.
Roast the peppers for 15 minutes until the skins blister and the pepper flesh becomes tender.
While the peppers roast, mince the garlic and mix it with the remaining tablespoon of olive oil to create a garlic-infused oil.
When the peppers are done, transfer them to a bowl and let them cool slightly, then cut them into bite-size cubes.
Add the diced avocado, chopped cilantro, and lime juice to the bowl of roasted peppers.
Drizzle the garlic-infused oil over the salad, season with a pinch of cayenne pepper if desired, then toss gently to combine.
Serve immediately, or chill in the refrigerator for 10 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Enjoy a crisp, garlicky Brazilian pepper salad that is both low-carb and diabetic-friendly.
